verses.latin_vulgate: Amen, amen dico vobis: qui non intrat per ostium in ovile ovium, sed ascendit aliunde, ille fur est et latro.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὁ μὴ εἰσερχόμενος διὰ τῆς θύρας εἰς τὴν αὐλὴν τῶν προβάτων ἀλλὰ ἀναβαίνων ἀλλαχόθεν ἐκεῖνος κλέπτης ἐστὶν καὶ λῃστής
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem intrat per ostium, pastor est ovium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἰσερχόμενος διὰ τῆς θύρας ποιμήν ἐστιν τῶν προβάτων
verses.king_james: But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Huic ostiarius aperit, et oves vocem ejus audiunt, et proprias ovas vocat nominatim, et educit eas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τούτῳ ὁ θυρωρὸς ἀνοίγει καὶ τὰ πρόβατα τῆς φωνῆς αὐτοῦ ἀκούει καὶ τὰ ἴδια πρόβατα καλεῖ κατ᾽ ὄνομα καὶ ἐξάγει αὐτά
verses.king_james: To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and leadeth them out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum proprias oves emiserit, ante eas vadit: et oves illum sequuntur, quia sciunt vocem ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅταν τὰ ἴδια πρόβατα ἐκβάλῃ ἔμπροσθεν αὐτῶν πορεύεται καὶ τὰ πρόβατα αὐτῷ ἀκολουθεῖ ὅτι οἴδασιν τὴν φωνὴν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they know his voice.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alienum autem non sequuntur, sed fugiunt ab eo: quia non noverunt vocem alienorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλοτρίῳ δὲ οὐ μὴ ἀκολουθήσωσιν, ἀλλὰ φεύξονται ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ ὅτι οὐκ οἴδασιν τῶν ἀλλοτρίων τὴν φωνήν
verses.king_james: And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of strangers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc proverbium dixit eis Jesus: illi autem non cognoverunt quid loqueretur eis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταύτην τὴν παροιμίαν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐκεῖνοι δὲ οὐκ ἔγνωσαν τίνα ἦν ἃ ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo eis iterum Jesus: Amen, amen dico vobis, quia ego sum ostium ovium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν πάλιν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ θύρα τῶν προβάτων
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the door of the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes quotquot venerunt, fures sunt, et latrones, et non audierunt eos oves.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντες ὅσοι πρὸ ἐμοῦ ἦλθον κλέπται εἰσὶν καὶ λῃσταί ἀλλ᾽ οὐκ ἤκουσαν αὐτῶν τὰ πρόβατα
verses.king_james: All that ever came before me are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum ostium. Per me si quis introierit, salvabitur: et ingredietur, et egredietur, et pascua inveniet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ θύρα δι᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐάν τις εἰσέλθῃ σωθήσεται καὶ εἰσελεύσεται καὶ ἐξελεύσεται καὶ νομὴν εὑρήσει
verses.king_james: I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fur non venit nisi ut furetur, et mactet, et perdat. Ego veni ut vitam habeant, et abundantius habeant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ κλέπτης οὐκ ἔρχεται εἰ μὴ ἵνα κλέψῃ καὶ θύσῃ καὶ ἀπολέσῃ ἐγὼ ἦλθον ἵνα ζωὴν ἔχωσιν καὶ περισσὸν ἔχωσιν
verses.king_james: The thief cometh not, but for to steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have life, and that they might have *it* more abundantly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum pastor bonus. Bonus pastor animam suam dat pro ovibus suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ καλός ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ καλὸς τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ τίθησιν ὑπὲρ τῶν προβάτων
verses.king_james: I am the good shepherd: the good shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mercenarius autem, et qui non est pastor, cujus non sunt oves propriæ, videt lupum venientem, et dimittit oves, et fugit: et lupus rapit, et dispergit oves;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ μισθωτὸς δὲ, καὶ οὐκ ὢν ποιμήν οὗ οὐκ εἰσιν τὰ πρόβατα ἴδια θεωρεῖ τὸν λύκον ἐρχόμενον καὶ ἀφίησιν τὰ πρόβατα καὶ φεύγει καὶ ὁ λύκος ἁρπάζει αὐτὰ καὶ σκορπίζει τὰ πρόβατα
verses.king_james: But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: mercenarius autem fugit, quia mercenarius est, et non pertinet ad eum de ovibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ μισθωτός φεύγει, ὅτι μισθωτὸς ἐστιν καὶ οὐ μέλει αὐτῷ περὶ τῶν προβάτων
verses.king_james: The hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum pastor bonus: et cognosco meas, et cognoscunt me meæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ καλός καὶ γινώσκω τὰ ἐμὰ καὶ γινώσκομαι ὑπὸ τῶν ἐμῶν
verses.king_james: I am the good shepherd, and know my *sheep*, and am known of mine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut novit me Pater, et ego agnosco Patrem: et animam meam pono pro ovibus meis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γινώσκει με ὁ πατὴρ κἀγὼ γινώσκω τὸν πατέρα καὶ τὴν ψυχήν μου τίθημι ὑπὲρ τῶν προβάτων
verses.king_james: As the Father knoweth me, even so know I the Father: and I lay down my life for the sheep.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et alias oves habeo, quæ non sunt ex hoc ovili: et illas oportet me adducere, et vocem meam audient, et fiet unum ovile et unus pastor.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἄλλα πρόβατα ἔχω ἃ οὐκ ἔστιν ἐκ τῆς αὐλῆς ταύτης κἀκεῖνα με δεῖ ἀγαγεῖν καὶ τῆς φωνῆς μου ἀκούσουσιν καὶ γενήσεται μία ποίμνη εἷς ποιμήν
verses.king_james: And other sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be one fold, *and* one shepherd.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea me diligit Pater: quia ego pono animam meam, ut iterum sumam eam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτό ὁ πατὴρ με ἀγαπᾷ ὅτι ἐγὼ τίθημι τὴν ψυχήν μου ἵνα πάλιν λάβω αὐτήν
verses.king_james: Therefore doth my Father love me, because I lay down my life, that I might take it again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo tollit eam a me: sed ego pono eam a meipso, et potestatem habeo ponendi eam, et potestatem habeo iterum sumendi eam. Hoc mandatum accepi a Patre meo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς αἴρει αὐτὴν ἀπ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ἐγὼ τίθημι αὐτὴν ἀπ᾽ ἐμαυτοῦ ἐξουσίαν ἔχω θεῖναι αὐτήν καὶ ἐξουσίαν ἔχω πάλιν λαβεῖν αὐτήν ταύτην τὴν ἐντολὴν ἔλαβον παρὰ τοῦ πατρός μου
verses.king_james: No man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have power to lay it down, and I have power to take it again. This commandment have I received of my Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissensio iterum facta est inter Judæos propter sermones hos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σχίσμα οὖν πάλιν ἐγένετο ἐν τοῖς ἰουδαίοις διὰ τοὺς λόγους τούτους
verses.king_james: There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant autem multi ex ipsis: Dæmonium habet, et insanit: quid eum auditis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον δὲ πολλοὶ ἐξ αὐτῶν δαιμόνιον ἔχει καὶ μαίνεται τί αὐτοῦ ἀκούετε
verses.king_james: And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Alii dicebant: Hæc verba non sunt dæmonium habentis: numquid dæmonium potest cæcorum oculos aperire?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄλλοι ἔλεγον ταῦτα τὰ ῥήματα οὐκ ἔστιν δαιμονιζομένου μὴ δαιμόνιον δύναται τυφλῶν ὀφθαλμοὺς ἀνοίγειν
verses.king_james: Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?

verses.latin_vulgate: Facta sunt autem Encænia in Jerosolymis, et hiems erat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δὲ τὰ ἐγκαίνια ἐν τοῖς ἱεροσολύμοις καὶ χειμὼν ἦν
verses.king_james: And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ambulabat Jesus in templo, in porticu Salomonis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ περιεπάτει ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ ἐν τῇ στοᾷ τοῦ σολομῶντος
verses.king_james: And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon's porch.

verses.latin_vulgate: Circumdederunt ergo eum Judæi, et dicebant ei: Quousque animam nostram tollis? si tu es Christus, dic nobis palam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκύκλωσαν οὖν αὐτὸν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι καὶ ἔλεγον αὐτῷ ἕως πότε τὴν ψυχὴν ἡμῶν αἴρεις εἰ σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστός εἰπὲ ἡμῖν παῤῥησίᾳ
verses.king_james: Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Jesus: Loquor vobis, et non creditis: opera quæ ego facio in nomine Patris mei, hæc testimonium perhibent de me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς εἶπον ὑμῖν καὶ οὐ πιστεύετε τὰ ἔργα ἃ ἐγὼ ποιῶ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματι τοῦ πατρός μου ταῦτα μαρτυρεῖ περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye believed not: the works that I do in my Father's name, they bear witness of me.

verses.latin_vulgate: sed vos non creditis, quia non estis ex ovibus meis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὑμεῖς οὐ πιστεύετε οὐ γὰρ ἐστὲ ἐκ τῶν προβάτων τῶν ἐμῶν καθὼς εἶπον ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oves meæ vocem meam audiunt, et ego cognosco eas, et sequuntur me:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ πρόβατα τὰ ἐμὰ τῆς φωνῆς μου ἀκούει, κἀγὼ γινώσκω αὐτά καὶ ἀκολουθοῦσίν μοι
verses.king_james: My sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they follow me:

verses.latin_vulgate: et ego vitam æternam do eis, et non peribunt in æternum, et non rapiet eas quisquam de manu mea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ ζωὴν αἰώνιον δίδωμι αὐτοῖς καὶ οὐ μὴ ἀπόλωνται εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα καὶ οὐχ ἁρπάσει τις αὐτὰ ἐκ τῆς χειρός μου
verses.king_james: And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any *man* pluck them out of my hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pater meus quod dedit mihi, majus omnibus est: et nemo potest rapere de manu Patris mei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πατήρ μου ὃς δέδωκέν μοι μεῖζών πάντων ἐστιν καὶ οὐδεὶς δύναται ἁρπάζειν ἐκ τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ πατρός μου
verses.king_james: My Father, which gave *them* me, is greater than all; and no *man* is able to pluck *them* out of my Father's hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego et Pater unum sumus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ἕν ἐσμεν
verses.king_james: I and *my* Father are one.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sustulerunt ergo lapides Judæi, ut lapidarent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐβάστασαν οὖν πάλιν λίθους οἱ ἰουδαῖοι ἵνα λιθάσωσιν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then the Jews took up stones again to stone him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Jesus: Multa bona opera ostendi vobis ex Patre meo: propter quod eorum opus me lapidatis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς πολλὰ καλὰ ἔργα ἔδειξα ὑμῖν ἐκ τοῦ πατρός μου διὰ ποῖον αὐτῶν ἔργον λιθάζετε μὲ
verses.king_james: Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ei Judæi: De bono opere non lapidamus te, sed de blasphemia; et quia tu homo cum sis, facis teipsum Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ οἱ ἰουδαῖοι λέγοντες, περὶ καλοῦ ἔργου οὐ λιθάζομέν σε ἀλλὰ περὶ βλασφημίας καὶ ὅτι σὺ ἄνθρωπος ὢν ποιεῖς σεαυτὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Jesus: Nonne scriptum est in lege vestra, Quia ego dixi: Dii estis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς οὐκ ἔστιν γεγραμμένον ἐν τῷ νόμῳ ὑμῶν ἐγὼ εἶπα θεοί ἐστε
verses.king_james: Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are gods?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si illos dixit deos, ad quos sermo Dei factus est, et non potest solvi Scriptura:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ἐκείνους εἶπεν θεοὺς πρὸς οὓς ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ ἐγένετο καὶ οὐ δύναται λυθῆναι ἡ γραφή
verses.king_james: If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;

verses.latin_vulgate: quem Pater sanctificavit, et misit in mundum vos dicitis: Quia blasphemas, quia dixi: Filius Dei sum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν ὁ πατὴρ ἡγίασεν καὶ ἀπέστειλεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον ὑμεῖς λέγετε ὅτι βλασφημεῖς ὅτι εἶπον Υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ εἰμι
verses.king_james: Say ye of him, whom the Father hath sanctified, and sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the Son of God?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si non facio opera Patris mei, nolite credere mihi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ οὐ ποιῶ τὰ ἔργα τοῦ πατρός μου μὴ πιστεύετέ μοι
verses.king_james: If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem facio: etsi mihi non vultis credere, operibus credite, ut cognoscatis, et credatis quia Pater in me est, et ego in Patre.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ποιῶ κἂν ἐμοὶ μὴ πιστεύητε τοῖς ἔργοις πιστεύσατε ἵνα γνῶτε καὶ πιστεύσητε ὅτι ἐν ἐμοὶ ὁ πατὴρ κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: But if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the Father *is* in me, and I in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quærebant ergo eum apprehendere: et exivit de manibus eorum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐζήτουν οὖν πάλιν αὐτὸν πιάσαι καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἐκ τῆς χειρὸς αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Therefore they sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et abiit iterum trans Jordanem, in eum locum ubi erat Joannes baptizans primum, et mansit illic;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν πάλιν πέραν τοῦ ἰορδάνου εἰς τὸν τόπον ὅπου ἦν ἰωάννης τὸ πρῶτον βαπτίζων καὶ ἔμεινεν ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.

verses.latin_vulgate: et multi venerunt ad eum, et dicebant: Quia Joannes quidem signum fecit nullum. Omnia autem quæcumque dixit Joannes de hoc, vera erant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πολλοὶ ἦλθον πρὸς αὐτὸν καὶ ἔλεγον ὅτι ἰωάννης μὲν σημεῖον ἐποίησεν οὐδέν πάντα δὲ ὅσα εἶπεν ἰωάννης περὶ τούτου ἀληθῆ ἦν
verses.king_james: And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et multi crediderunt in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπίστευσαν πολλοὶ ἐκεῖ εἰς αὐτὸν
verses.king_james: And many believed on him there.
